page: Amazon Web Services c6gd.medium is an ARM64-based compute-optimized server instance powered by a dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor. It features 1 physical core and 1 vCPU running at 2.5 GHz, supported by 2.0 GB of system memory. The CPU cache configuration includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. This instance is equipped with 59 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses, and features a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates that the c6gd.medium delivers average single-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1490.59. However, due to its single-core configuration, multi-core performance is limited, matching the single-core stress-ng score and placing it in the lower performance tiers. Memory bandwidth benchmarks show 59.39 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, dropping to 20.1 GB/sec for large-block reads and 9.8 GB/sec for large-block writes. Cryptographic capabilities are supported by hardware AES acceleration, though other advanced instruction sets like AVX or AMX are absent.

The primary tradeoff of the c6gd.medium is its highly constrained resource footprint, featuring only 1 vCPU and 2.0 GB of RAM, which limits its utility for parallel processing or memory-intensive tasks. This is reflected in its lower benchmark scores for database operations, web serving throughput of 1.14 GB/sec, and LLM inference of 29.22 tokens/sec for text generation on a 135M model. However, the inclusion of 59 GB of local NVMe SSD storage provides high-speed local read and write capabilities. This makes the instance qualitatively cost-efficient for lightweight, single-threaded workloads, basic edge processing, simple static web serving, and small-scale ARM64 development or testing environments that benefit from dedicated CPU allocation.
description: Amazon Web Services c6gd.medium is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring a single dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 core running at 2.5 GHz, paired with 2.0 GB of memory and 59 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this instance delivers average single-core CPU performance with a stress-ng score of 1490.59, though its single-core design limits multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth. The hardware profile lacks GPU acceleration and advanced vector extensions but includes hardware AES cryptography. With a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps, the c6gd.medium represents a low-resource, entry-level compute option. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for lightweight single-threaded applications, basic static web serving, and ARM64 software development or testing where local high-speed SSD storage is required but massive memory or multi-core processing is unnecessary.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6gd.medium is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance with 1 Graviton vCPU, 2.0 GB of RAM, and 59 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, designed for lightweight single-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6gd.medium is an ARM64 instance with 1 Graviton vCPU, 2.0 GB RAM, and 59 GB NVMe SSD storage for lightweight workloads.
tagline: An entry-level ARM64 compute instance featuring a dedicated Graviton core and high-speed local NVMe SSD storage for lightweight workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a dedicated 2.5 GHz AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 ARM64 processor
Equipped with 2.0 GB of system memory and 59 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Delivers average single-core performance but limited multi-core capacity
Features hardware-accelerated AES cryptography on the Nitro hypervisor
Best suited for lightweight single-threaded tasks, static web serving, and ARM64 testing
